title: ArchivedChangeIndexEntry
description: Listing archived changes must remain fast even when the archive contains many entries. `ArchivedChangeIndexEntry` defines the lightweight, index-backed record returned by archive listing APIs without requiring a `manifest.json` read per archived change.
context: Listing archived changes must remain fast even when the archive contains many entries. `ArchivedChangeIndexEntry` defines the lightweight, index-backed record returned by archive listing APIs without requiring a `manifest.json` read per archived change.

rules:
An `ArchivedChangeIndexEntry` MUST include the following fields:

- `name` (string) — original change name (lookup key)
- `archivedName` (string) — archive directory name
- `archivedAt` (Date) — archive timestamp
- `archivedBy` (ActorIdentity, optional) — actor identity when recorded
- `artifacts` (string\[]) — artifact type IDs present at archive time
- `specIds` (string\[]) — spec IDs associated with the change at archive time
- `schemaName` (string) and `schemaVersion` (number) — governing schema identity

`ArchivedChangeIndexEntry` MUST NOT require access to `manifest.json`-only fields that are not reliably present in `index.jsonl` entries (for example detailed artifact state maps or full change history).
`ArchivedChangeIndexEntry` SHOULD provide `workspaces` derived from `specIds` (workspace prefixes before `:`) for display and filtering.

constraints: ArchivedChangeIndexEntry is a read-only data model with no mutating operations.
It is intentionally incomplete; full archived change inspection is provided by ArchivedChange loaded from the archive manifest.

scenarios.name: Index entry contains required fields
given: an archive index entry loaded from index.jsonl
when: it is represented as an ArchivedChangeIndexEntry
then: it contains name, archivedName, archivedAt, specIds, schemaName, and schemaVersion
AND it may contain archivedBy when recorded
AND it contains artifacts as an array of artifact type IDs

scenarios.name: Index entry is sufficient for listing without manifest reads
when: an archive listing is produced from ArchivedChangeIndexEntry values
then: the listing does not require loading manifest.json for every entry

scenarios.name: Workspaces are derived from specIds
given: an ArchivedChangeIndexEntry with specIds containing core:change and cli:archive-show

optimizedDescription: Lightweight archive listing record: name, path, derived workspaces, no manifest-only data load.
optimizedContext:
# ArchivedChangeIndexEntry

## Rules
- Index entry fields: `ArchivedChangeIndexEntry` MUST include following fields: `name` (string) — original change name (lookup key) `archivedName` (string) — archive directory name `archivedAt` (Date) — archive timestamp `archivedBy` (ActorIdentity, optional) — actor identity when recorded `artifacts` (string\[]) — artifact type IDs present at archive time `specIds` (string\[]) — spec IDs associated with change at archive time `schemaName` (string) and `schemaVersion` (number) — governing schema identity
- No manifest-only data: `ArchivedChangeIndexEntry` MUST NOT require access to `manifest.json`-only fields are not reliably present in `index.jsonl` entries (for example detailed artifact state maps or full change history).
- Derived workspaces: `ArchivedChangeIndexEntry` SHOULD provide `workspaces` derived from `specIds` (workspace prefixes before `:`) for display and filtering.

## Constraints
- ArchivedChangeIndexEntry is read-only data model with no mutating operations.
- It is intentionally incomplete; full archived change inspection is provided by ArchivedChange loaded from archive manifest.
